data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-d9a0ed42">
    <title>agent: add downgrade-loop regression coverage</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The downgrade-loop failure needs an end-to-end regression guard. Unit tests for individual helpers are not enough; a future change could still reintroduce an auto-downgrade across two ddx work/watch passes after operator promotion.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1037-1041 documents that terminal non-actionable readiness outcomes unclaim and park so the bead does not reappear in ReadyExecution until operator review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 implements that park path. Without a full loop test, a promoted open bead can be parked again by the same readiness result.
- cli/cmd/agent_run_passthrough_test.go and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop*_test.go contain readiness/proposed assertions today, but no test proves proposed -> open operator acceptance survives repeated worker passes for the same finding while hard manual-required cases remain parked.

PROPOSED FIX
Add regression coverage that exercises the real loop shape:
- Create or seed an open bead.
- Simulate/readiness-return a parkable finding and assert first pass parks to proposed with evidence.
- Promote proposed -> open through the supported bead lifecycle path.
- Run at least two additional worker/readiness passes with the same finding and assert the bead does not re-enter proposed.
- Run a separate hard manual-required scenario that must still park to proposed, so continuous-forward-progress does not become agent guessing.

NON-SCOPE
- No new production policy beyond what ddx-91898f19, ddx-cd69c24, and ddx-78ab5d61 implement.
- No live Fizeau or external model calls; use existing test runner/hook stubs.
- No changes to unrelated post-land review tests except updating expected event bodies if needed.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on ddx-91898f19, ddx-cd69c24, and ddx-78ab5d61.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Add TestWorkLoopOperatorPromotedOpenDoesNotDowngradeAgain: first readiness pass parks an open bead to proposed, supported lifecycle promotion moves it back to open, and two later loop passes with the same finding leave it non-proposed.
2. Add TestWorkLoopOperatorOverridePreservesCommitmentText asserting title, description, acceptance criteria, labels, parent, and deps are not lost or silently rewritten while honoring the override.
3. Add TestWorkLoopHardManualRequiredStillParks asserting contradictory/unsafe operator_required readiness still parks to proposed even after the WARN-ONLY changes.
4. The regression uses the same execute loop/readiness hook path as ddx work, not only a pure helper.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkLoop(OperatorPromotedOpenDoesNotDowngradeAgain|OperatorOverridePreservesCommitmentText|HardManualRequiredStillParks)"` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:bead-lifecycle, area:tests, kind:test, prevention, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-cc0641a7" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="f29684199a1f171bf7f0332a6aa42ffaf2eca8ac" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

- Lint cleanups applied in [candidate_cycle.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d9a0ed42-20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba/cli/internal/agent/candidate_cycle.go) and [execute_bead_loop.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d9a0ed42-20260512T181845-0bd7d7ba/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- Commit: `7ce1b8c42 test: add downgrade-loop regression coverage [ddx-d9a0ed42]`

Verification passed:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkLoop(OperatorPromotedOpenDoesNotDowngradeAgain|OperatorOverridePreservesCommitmentText|HardManualRequiredStillParks)'`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`
